dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6392 RH: Home School Graduation Recognition Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Harris of North Carolina (for himself and Mrs. Miller of Illinois) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce
Additional sponsors: Mrs. Harshbarger, Mr. Steube, Mr. Gosar, Mr. Fine, Mr. Kiley of California, Mr. Evans of Colorado, Mrs. Houchin, and Mr. McGuire
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on December 3, 2025

form.official-title: To amend the Higher Education Act of 1965 to recognize students who have completed secondary school education in a home school setting as high school graduates, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Home School Graduation Recognition Act.

section.header: Recognizing home school graduates as high school graduates
section.text: Section 484(d) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1091(d)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the heading of such subsection, by striking Who Are Not High School Graduates and inserting From Non-Traditional Settings; and
by adding at the end the following:

For purposes of this Act, a student who has completed a secondary school education in a home school setting that is treated as a home school or private school under State law shall be considered a high school graduate.

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
